text: The provided topological_ordering function is incorrect because it does not correctly order nodes according to their dependencies. The function tries to form ordered_nodes by starting from nodes without incoming edges, but the loop condition and logic for adding next nodes are flawed. This leads to incomplete and improperly ordered results, as shown by failing test cases where nodes dependent on others are missing or appear out of order.

data.label: topological_ordering does not return nodes in correct topological order
data.source: test outputs and asserts failing with incorrect order and missing nodes

data.label: Test failures show output missing nodes and nodes appearing before their dependencies
Code logic uses incorrect condition `set(ordered_nodes).issuperset(nextnode.outgoing_nodes)` to add nodes
Correct topological sorting requires nodes to appear before all their outgoing nodes
